dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The worn-out court behind Seoyun High was Ji-Hoon's sanctuary. Every day, after everyone else had gone home, he was here.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Ji-Hoon
dialogue.line: Just one more.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Ji-Hoon
dialogue.line: (My hands are numb, my legs are burning. But the feeling of the ball... it's everything.)

dialogue.speaker: sfx
dialogue.line: BOUNCE. BOUNCE. BOUNCE.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Ji-Hoon
dialogue.line: (They call me 'bench warmer' for a reason. Too short, too slow, they say. But they don't see this.)

dialogue.speaker: sfx
dialogue.line: WHOOSH!

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: He launched a three-pointer from deep, a perfect arc. The ball kissed the rim, hesitated, and then... spun out.

dialogue.speaker: sfx
dialogue.line: CLANG!

dialogue.speaker: Kim Ji-Hoon
dialogue.line: Damn it!

dialogue.speaker: sfx
dialogue.line: PANT. PANT.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Ji-Hoon
dialogue.line: (Another miss. How many has it been today? A hundred? Two hundred? It doesn't matter. I have to make it.)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: He grabbed the rebound, dribbling instinctively, his eyes scanning an imaginary defense. He wasn't just shooting; he was playing a full game in his head.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Ji-Hoon
dialogue.line: Screen left... drive baseline... crossover!

dialogue.speaker: sfx
dialogue.line: THWACK! (Ball hitting the ground hard)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: He faked a pass, then pulled up sharply, fading away from an invisible defender. It was a shot only a true shooter would attempt.

dialogue.speaker: sfx
dialogue.line: SWISH!

dialogue.speaker: Kim Ji-Hoon
dialogue.line: Yes!

dialogue.speaker: Kim Ji-Hoon
dialogue.line: (That feeling... the net swaying, the perfect silence after the shot. It's addicting.)

dialogue.speaker: Coach Lee
dialogue.line: Not bad, Ji-Hoon.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Ji-Hoon
dialogue.line: Coach Lee?!

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Ji-Hoon froze, his face flushing. He hadn't realized anyone was watching.

dialogue.speaker: Coach Lee
dialogue.line: You're still here. Every day. I've been watching.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Ji-Hoon
dialogue.line: I... I just like to practice, Coach.

dialogue.speaker: Coach Lee
dialogue.line: You've got a good shot. A real touch. But you lack consistency. And confidence.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Ji-Hoon
dialogue.line: (He knows. He sees it all.)

dialogue.speaker: Coach Lee
dialogue.line: The tryouts for the main team are next week. You're still on the reserve list, right?

dialogue.speaker: Kim Ji-Hoon
dialogue.line: Yes, Coach.

dialogue.speaker: Coach Lee
dialogue.line: I saw you hesitate during the last scrimmage. You had an open look, but you passed. Why?

dialogue.speaker: Kim Ji-Hoon
dialogue.line: I... I thought Min-Joon had a better angle.

dialogue.speaker: Coach Lee
dialogue.line: Min-Joon is a great player, but this isn't about him. It's about you. When you have the shot, you take it. That's what a 'winning shot' is.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Ji-Hoon
dialogue.line: (A winning shot... I've always dreamed of making one. The one that decides the game.)

dialogue.speaker: Coach Lee
dialogue.line: Show me that shot you just made. The fadeaway. Right now. Imagine it's the final second, game on the line.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Ji-Hoon felt a surge of adrenaline. This wasn't just practice anymore. This was his chance.

dialogue.speaker: sfx
dialogue.line: BOUNCE. BOUNCE.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Ji-Hoon
dialogue.line: (Final second... game on the line... my shot.)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: He drove hard, planted his foot, and launched himself upwards, leaning back, the ball leaving his fingertips with a prayer.

dialogue.speaker: sfx
dialogue.line: WHOOSH!

dialogue.speaker: sfx
dialogue.line: SWISH!

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The net rippled. Pure. Undefended. The sound echoed in the twilight.

dialogue.speaker: Coach Lee
dialogue.line: Good. Now do it when it counts. Next week.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Coach Lee turned and walked away, leaving Ji-Hoon standing under the hoop, the ball still bouncing gently. A new fire ignited in his chest.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Ji-Hoon
dialogue.line: (Next week. I'll show them. I'll make my winning shot.)

summary: Kim Ji-Hoon, an undersized basketball enthusiast, secretly practices his impressive shooting skills on a deserted court. When Coach Lee observes his talent but also his lack of confidence, Ji-Hoon is challenged to make his 'winning shot' during the upcoming tryouts for the main team.
